hero.tagline: From Ancient Scrolls to Every Language
hero.title: The Bible Translation Timeline
hero.subtitle: A journey through 3,400 years of history — from the first Hebrew texts to the projected day when every language on Earth will have access to Scripture.

chart.title: The Exponential Growth of Translation
chart.subtitle: Number of languages with Scripture over time. The dashed region represents projections based on current acceleration trends.

countdown.title: Countdown to Every Language
countdown.subtitle: Based on the 2022–2025 translation rhythm with projected 10% annual acceleration from AI and global collaboration.
countdown.timeRemaining: Time remaining until Full Bible in every language (~2062)
countdown.microTagline: Estimated progress since you opened this page
countdown.microExplain: This counter estimates real-time progress based on the average rate of ~{{rate}} new languages gaining Scripture per day (with 10% annual acceleration). The actual work happens in bursts — translation teams complete projects over months and years — but this gives a sense of the ongoing momentum.
countdown.methodology: Methodology: Baseline data from Wycliffe Global Alliance reports (2022–2025). Annual rates: ~17.3 Full Bibles, ~60.3 New Testaments, ~139.3 total languages gaining Scripture. Acceleration model assumes 10% compound annual growth driven by AI tools (AQuA, Greek Room) and expanded global collaboration. These are illustrative estimates, not precise predictions.

closing.title: From One Language to Every Language
closing.text: What began with a single scroll in ancient Hebrew has grown into the largest translation project in human history. With AI acceleration and global collaboration, the goal of reaching every language is closer than ever — projected by 2062 for a Full Bible, and by the mid-2030s for at least some Scripture in every tongue.
closing.verse: Through God we shall do valiantly.

footer.disclaimer: Projections are estimates based on 2022–2025 translation rates and should be interpreted as illustrative, not definitive.

ancient.title: The Ancient Texts
ancient.subtitle: The original Scriptures are written and first translated

early-church.title: The Early Church
early-church.subtitle: Scripture spreads across the Roman world and beyond

medieval.title: The Medieval Era
medieval.subtitle: Translations preserved through centuries of upheaval

reformation.title: Reformation & Print
reformation.subtitle: The printing press ignites a translation revolution

missions.title: The Missionary Age
missions.subtitle: Global missions carry the Bible to every continent

modern.title: The Modern Movement
modern.subtitle: Systematic translation efforts transform the landscape

acceleration.title: The Great Acceleration
acceleration.subtitle: Technology and collaboration drive exponential growth

future.title: The Projected Future
future.subtitle: When will every language have Scripture?

torah.title: The Torah is Written
torah.description: The earliest books of the Hebrew Bible begin to be composed. These texts, written in Hebrew, form the foundation of all Scripture that will follow.

septuagint.title: The Septuagint
septuagint.description: Jewish scholars in Alexandria translate the Hebrew Old Testament into Greek, creating the Septuagint. This becomes the most widely used version of the Old Testament in the early Christian church.

nt-written.title: The New Testament is Written
nt-written.description: The Gospels, letters of Paul, and other New Testament books are composed in Koine Greek, the common language of the Eastern Mediterranean.

peshitta.title: The Syriac Peshitta
peshitta.description: The first translation of Scripture in the Christian era is completed in Syriac, the language spoken in Damascus. The early Syrian Church becomes a center for mission and translation.

latin-coptic.title: Latin & Coptic Translations
latin-coptic.description: Early Latin translations (Vetus Latina) appear in North Africa and Europe. Coptic translations bring Scripture to Egypt. The Bible begins spreading beyond Greek-speaking communities.

vulgate.title: Jerome's Vulgate
vulgate.description: Commissioned by Pope Damasus, Jerome produces a fresh Latin translation from Hebrew and Greek sources. The Vulgate becomes the standard Bible of Western Christianity for over a thousand years.

armenian.title: Armenian, Georgian & Ethiopic
armenian.description: Translations appear in Armenian (c. 405), Georgian (c. 430), and Ge'ez (Ethiopic). Bible translation activity spreads from Syria into Armenia, Georgia, and beyond.

cyril.title: Cyril & Methodius
cyril.description: The missionary brothers Cyril and Methodius translate Scripture into Old Church Slavonic, creating the Glagolitic alphabet to do so. This opens the Bible to the Slavic peoples of Eastern Europe.

anglo-saxon.title: Anglo-Saxon Translations
anglo-saxon.description: The Venerable Bede translates the Gospel of John into Old English. Over the following centuries, partial translations and paraphrases appear across England.

wycliffe-bible.title: Wycliffe's English Bible
wycliffe-bible.description: John Wycliffe produces the first complete Bible in English, translated from the Latin Vulgate. Hand-copied by his followers, it sparks a movement for ordinary people to read Scripture.

gutenberg.title: The Gutenberg Bible
gutenberg.description: Johannes Gutenberg's printing press produces the first major printed book — a Latin Bible. This invention transforms Bible distribution from hand-copying to mass production.

erasmus.title: Erasmus's Greek New Testament
erasmus.description: Erasmus of Rotterdam publishes the first printed edition of the Greek New Testament. Scholars across Europe can now work directly from the original Greek text.

luther.title: Luther's German New Testament
luther.description: Martin Luther translates the New Testament into German while hiding in Wartburg Castle. His translation becomes a cornerstone of the Protestant Reformation.

tyndale.title: Tyndale's English New Testament
tyndale.description: William Tyndale produces the first printed English New Testament, translated directly from Greek. He coins phrases like 'at-one-ment' that endure to this day. Tyndale is later executed for his work.

15-languages.title: 15 European Languages
15-languages.description: By the end of the 16th century, the entire Bible has been printed in 15 European languages. The printing press and Reformation zeal have transformed the translation landscape.

kjv.title: The King James Version
kjv.description: Commissioned by King James I of England, 47 scholars produce the Authorized Version. Retaining much of Tyndale's work, it becomes the most influential English Bible for over 300 years.

bfbs.title: British & Foreign Bible Society
bfbs.description: The founding of the BFBS marks the beginning of organized global Bible distribution. It sparks the creation of similar societies worldwide and accelerates translation into non-European languages.

great-century.title: The Great Century of Missions
great-century.description: Missionary movements carry the Bible to Africa, Asia, and the Pacific. By 1900, the Bible or portions exist in over 500 languages.

crowther.title: Crowther's Yoruba Bible
crowther.description: Samuel Ajayi Crowther, a formerly enslaved man who became the first African Anglican bishop, completes the Yoruba Bible — one of the most influential Bible translations in African history.

wycliffe-founded.title: Wycliffe Bible Translators Founded
wycliffe-founded.description: William Cameron Townsend founds Wycliffe Bible Translators in Mexico, dedicated to translating the Bible into every language that needs it. This marks the beginning of the modern, systematic Bible translation movement.

sil.title: SIL International Established
sil.description: The Summer Institute of Linguistics (SIL) is formally established as the academic partner of Wycliffe, providing linguistic research and translation science to support Bible translation worldwide.

ubs.title: United Bible Societies Alliance
ubs.description: The United Bible Societies (UBS) formalizes a global network of Bible Societies working in over 200 countries. Together with Wycliffe/SIL, they form the backbone of the modern translation movement.

vision2025.title: Vision 2025 Adopted
vision2025.description: Wycliffe and SIL adopt Vision 2025: a bold goal to have a Bible translation program in progress for every language that still needs one by 2025. Over 5,000 languages still have no Scripture.

digital.title: The Digital Revolution Begins
digital.description: Digital tools begin transforming Bible translation. Computer-assisted translation, digital lexicons, and online collaboration platforms allow translators to work faster and more accurately.

illuminations.title: IllumiNations Alliance Formed
illuminations.description: The world's largest Bible translation agencies form the IllumiNations alliance, pooling resources, data, and funding to eliminate 'Bible poverty.' Their goal: reach every language by 2033.

2020-waiting.title: 2,000 Languages Still Waiting
2020-waiting.description: Despite decades of progress, over 2,000 languages still have no Bible translation work underway. However, the pace of new translation starts is accelerating dramatically.

2022-record.title: Record-Breaking Progress
2022-record.description: A record-breaking year for Bible translation. 57 first translations are published by Bible Societies alone. AI-assisted tools begin entering the translation workflow.

2023-97pct.title: 97% of People Reached
2023-97pct.description: Up to 97% of the world's population now has access to at least some Scripture in their first language. Translation work is active in 3,283 languages across 167 countries.

2024-under1000.title: Under 1,000 Languages Waiting
2024-under1000.description: For the first time in history, fewer than 1,000 languages remain on the waiting list for Bible translation to begin. AI tools like AQuA are accelerating the process.

2025-99pct.title: 99% of People Reached
2025-99pct.description: Over 99% of the world's population has access to at least some Scripture. Only 544 languages remain on the waiting list. Wycliffe is engaged in 2,918 language projects — a 36% increase in one year.

proj-2027.title: Translation Started in Every Language
proj-2027.description: At the current rate of reduction (450 languages/year removed from the waiting list), Bible translation work will have been started in every known living language that needs it.

proj-2033.title: IllumiNations 2033 Goal
proj-2033.description: The IllumiNations alliance targets 2033 for: 100% of languages with at least some Scripture, 99.9% with a New Testament, and 95% with a Full Bible. This is the most ambitious benchmark in the Bible translation movement.

proj-2036.title: Some Scripture in Every Language
proj-2036.description: With AI-accelerated translation (assuming a 10% annual increase in translation speed), every vital language could have at least some Scripture portions by the mid-2030s.

proj-2041.title: Some Scripture (Linear Projection)
proj-2041.description: If the current average rate of 139 new languages per year continues without acceleration, every vital language would have at least some Scripture by approximately 2041.

proj-2048.title: New Testament in Every Language (Accelerated)
proj-2048.description: With continued AI acceleration, every vital language could have a complete New Testament by the late 2040s.

proj-2062.title: Full Bible in Every Language (Accelerated)
proj-2062.description: The most optimistic projection: with sustained AI acceleration and global collaboration, every vital language could have a complete Bible by the early 2060s. Without acceleration, this milestone would not be reached until well into the 2300s.
